concerns.text: Task T039 implements a Likelihood Ratio Test (LRT) to compare linear vs. quadratic models. However, FR-016 and SC-012 explicitly require reporting the p-value of the Gini² term itself from the quadratic regression model. An LRT p-value indicates model fit improvement, not the specific statistical significance of the Gini² coefficient. This is a semantic weakening of the non-linearity test requirement.

Task T016 calls `normalize_path` but does not explicitly mandate the 'word-boundary match' logic required by FR-009. FR-009 defines the Bug-File Proximity Heuristic as an 'exact match of the full filename... (word-boundary match)'. Without a specific task to implement this boundary logic (e.g., ensuring 'main.py' in text matches 'src/main.py' only if the path is fully present), the task risks implementing a substring match, violating the strict heuristic definition.

While T024 and T028 address excluding deleted modules for predictor and outcome respectively, there is no explicit verification task ensuring the temporal alignment of these exclusions (T vs T+1 windows) within a single run. FR-008 requires strict temporal separation to prevent survivorship bias. The absence of an integration test (e.g., in T033) validating that the T+1 bug window correctly aligns with the T ownership window for the *same* set of excluded modules creates a risk of silent data leakage.
